refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR includes multiple obligations: analysis of terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation. To ensure atomicity and clarity, it is necessary to split these into separate sub-FRs, each focusing on a single aspect of worksite area analysis. This avoids conflating multiple requirements into one and maintains solution neutrality by focusing on what the system must do, not how.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate and provide a representation of the terrain within the worksite area, independent of obstacle or work-zone data.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including obstacle representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including work-zone representation.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital terrain model representing the physical features of the worksite
Extract and represent key site features relevant to planning and execution
Integrate terrain and site features into a unified worksite model/map
Provide an interface to share the worksite model/map with planning and execution functions
selected_dp.selected_dp: Integrate terrain and site features into a unified worksite model/map

potential_sources: Integration of terrain and site features into a single model may create dependencies between data sources
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically groups terrain representation and site feature extraction into a unified model, supporting independence from specific technologies and enabling modular updates.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate terrain and site feature data
Risk that integration complexity may increase if data sources are heterogeneous
Assumes planning and execution functions can consume the unified model format
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly separating terrain modeling and site feature extraction as distinct DPs if data sources or update frequencies differ significantly, to reduce coupling and improve modularity.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the integration and processing of environmental data, including terrain and site features, to create a unified worksite model/map.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives sensor data and sends processed environmental model updates.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communicates integrated worksite model to other system modules and external systems.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as terrain and site feature integration logically belongs here; minimal diffusion as data is processed internally and shared via defined interfaces.
Low containment since vehicle focuses on mobility and control; integrating terrain data here would cause high diffusion to other modules.
Teleoperation is primarily for remote control; integrating terrain mapping would diffuse functionality and increase coupling.
Safety Services may use terrain data for hazard detection but should not own the integration; moderate diffusion expected.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for integrating terrain and site features into a unified worksite model because it centralizes environmental data processing, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data which may affect integration quality.
If NGHE Solution grows too large, it may become a bottleneck or single point of failure.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Integrate terrain and site features into a unified worksite model/map' directly addresses the FR of providing worksite area analysis including terrain representation. The DP logically fulfills the functional requirement by specifying the creation of a unified model/map that represents the terrain and site features.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk identified as the DP is contained within the NGHE Solution module with a low diffusion score of 2.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and containment score (8) for this DP, indicating appropriate architectural packaging. Other modules have low containment and high diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the implementation boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the NGHE Solution module receives telemetry data and communicates the integrated worksite model to other system modules, enabling validation against acceptance criteria such as availability of the worksite model to planning/execution functions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility to the NGHE Solution module with well-defined interfaces (Telemetry and Network Link) supports traceability from FR through DP to IM.

recommended_revisions: Monitor the dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data to mitigate integration quality risks.
Consider modularizing the NGHE Solution internally if it grows too large to avoid bottlenecks or single points of failure.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification and minimal architectural risks. The DP logically fulfills the FR, and the IM module (NGHE Solution) shows high cohesion and containment with low diffusion risk. Verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed. Given the high safety criticality, the current decomposition is sufficiently detailed to proceed with implementation and verification without further sub-FR decomposition at this stage.

revise.why: Monitor dependency on telemetry data and consider internal modularization of the NGHE Solution module to mitigate integration risks and avoid bottlenecks.

assumptions: Telemetry data will be timely and accurate as required.
NGHE Solution module will maintain high cohesion and low diffusion as system evolves.
open_risks: Dependency on telemetry data quality and timeliness.
Potential growth of NGHE Solution module leading to complexity or bottlenecks.
evidence_needed: Verification results confirming integrated worksite model availability and accuracy.
Monitoring data on module cohesion and diffusion over time.
